headline: Tech's worst week in a year - AI momentum breaks
detail: The market is repricing AI growth expectations hard, hitting chip and infrastructure names directly. AVGO and SMH sit squarely in the crosshairs - this isn't noise, it's the thesis being questioned. On margin, a continued unwind cuts deeper than it looks on paper.

headline: Oracle suffers worst week since 2001 dot-com bust
detail: AI financing concerns are now spilling into hardware and data-center adjacent names, not just software. This is the same sentiment headwind pressuring AVGO, which depends on AI infrastructure spending holding up. If institutional money keeps rotating out, your largest losing position has further to fall.

headline: U.S. strikes Iran after ceasefire violation
detail: A confirmed retaliatory strike is pulling oil prices higher in after-hours trading, adding a macro risk layer on top of the tech selloff. Spiking oil raises input costs and can dampen risk appetite broadly, which hits leveraged, concentrated tech books hardest. Watch how futures open - this could set the tone for Monday.

headline: Micron sinks 6% in wild week of trading
detail: Micron is a direct read-through for the semiconductor complex - when it cracks, SMH and chip-adjacent names feel it. Your SMH is still in the green but this kind of sector pressure can erode that cushion fast. It also reinforces that the chip trade is in a rough patch, not just a one-day wobble.
